notes: Single-event JSONL containing only a pre-compaction memory flush prompt — no tool_use events, no real user prompts, 15 seconds wall clock. Registry BUILD classification is wrong; this is a system-generated artefact, not a work session. The embedded conversation reveals the prior session was brain-dynamous setup work: Google OAuth for Gmail/Calendar, heartbeat.py testing, sqlite_vec fix, and architecture discussion around HEARTBEAT.md vs cron vs Supabase pg_cron. brain-dynamous is David's personal ambient AI OS (Python, uv, Claude Agent SDK, heartbeat-based reasoning, memory indexing). Key classifier finding: pre-compaction flush events must be detected early (look for 'Pre-compaction memory flush' in first user_prompt) and short-circuited to meta.compaction_flush before any tool-pattern analysis runs. Duration ≤30s is a secondary signal. Embedded conversation text has secondary attribution value if the prior session JSONL is missing.
